danger_signals.visual_form: Distinct enemy sprites (Skull, Medusa, Rocky, snake-like creatures)
danger_signals.meaning: Immediate threat to Lolo; contact results in life loss or movement impedance
Puzzle incomplete; cannot progress without collecting hearts and opening chest

danger_signals.visual_form: Empty heart collection indicators in room
danger_signals.meaning: Required gems not yet obtained; exit inaccessible
Environmental obstacle requiring bridge-building or enemy-egg use to cross
Environmental barrier; some require special power-ups to destroy

safety_signals.visual_form: Gem sprite appearing in treasure chest
safety_signals.meaning: Puzzle solved; progression unlocked and exit available

safety_signals.visual_form: Enemies disappear visually after gem collection
safety_signals.meaning: All enemies neutralized; safe to exit

safety_signals.visual_form: Distinct item sprites within heart frames (smash-rock power, bridge-build ability)
safety_signals.meaning: Temporary ability granted; enables obstacle traversal or enemy control

safety_signals.visual_form: Enemy sprite encased/transformed into egg visual
safety_signals.meaning: Enemy temporarily neutralized; can be pushed, used as bridge, or cleared
Incremental progress toward puzzle completion; necessary for chest unlock

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Appears in all rooms; represents player agency and rescue mission narrative

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Referenced in narrative premise; visual presence at castle endpoint
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Implied presence at King Egger's castle; represents goal state

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Castle structure represents domain and threat
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Enemies (Skull, Medusa, Rocky) serve as visual manifestation of Egger's control
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Multi-floor castle maze structure; 10 floors with 5 rooms each = 50 puzzle chambers as fortress layout

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Distinct sprite designs per enemy type
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Varying movement and attack patterns visible through animation frames
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Populate castle rooms; signal Egger's defensive strategy and maze danger

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Enemies present in room; hearts uncollected; chest locked
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemies patrol/attack; Lolo can restart at cost of life; exit blocked

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Fewer heart indicators empty; chest remains locked
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Lolo continues to face enemies; puzzle unsolved state persists

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Chest opens; gem visible and collectable; enemies vanish from screen
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Exit becomes accessible; all enemy threats neutralized; progression enabled

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Transition to next floor level; visual progression through castle structure
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Lolo advances deeper into King Egger's castle; narrative progression toward rescue

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Enemy sprite transforms into egg visual state
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemy immobilized temporarily; can be pushed or re-engaged; tactical advantage gained

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Lolo sprite disappears; life counter decrements; return to room start or Game Over
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player can restart screen at life cost; puzzle attempt reset

environmental_storytelling.example: 10-floor structure with increasing complexity implies deeper danger/challenge as Lolo descends toward Lala's location; visual ascent through castle levels shows tangible progress toward goal

Different enemy creatures (Skull, Medusa, Rocky) appear in thematic room arrangements; varying movement patterns (movement/attack variance) create escalating puzzle difficulty without text explanation
Water barriers, rock obstacles, and enemy-specific traits (freezing enemies, immobile blockers) introduce mechanic variety visually; each room's layout silently teaches new puzzle interaction rules

50-room journey through one villain's castle (King Egger's domain) = implicit world-state: Egger controls a vast maze-fortress; Lolo's penetration through it = narrative momentum toward rescue confrontation
Power-up items hidden in heart frames = resource scarcity and discovery; smash-rock and bridge-build abilities appear where needed, teaching problem-solving through visual layout logic

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Moves, slides blocks, fires limited shots; can be shot by enemies causing life loss
Collects hearts incrementally; navigates obstacles; hunts for gem pickup
Patrols maze; attacks on contact; encasable in egg when shot
Distinct movement/attack pattern (unspecified in sources); responds to Lolo's shots
Obstacle with distinct movement pattern; some enemies of this type immune to Lolo's shots
Visible opponent sprite type; encasable or defeatable via puzzle mechanics
Transforms into egg visual; immobilized temporarily; can be pushed as bridge or re-shot to remove
Disappears entirely from room; threat eliminated; exit safe
Remains locked; visual closure blocks exit; puzzles prevent opening
Opens; gem becomes visible and collectable; exit unlocked

notes: Adventures of Lolo is a pure puzzle-action game with minimal narrative exposition. World-state communication is entirely visual and behavioral: enemies manifest threat through sprite presence and attack animation, puzzle completion is signaled through environmental state changes (chest locking/unlocking, enemy disappearance), and progression through the 50-room castle is the primary narrative vehicle. The game uses no text overlays or dialogue; all danger/safety/faction information is encoded in sprite design, spatial layout, and mechanic response. No political factions exist; the conflict is purely Lolo vs. King Egger's castle maze. Environmental storytelling relies on puzzle room design logic (heart placement, enemy positioning, obstacle arrangement) to teach mechanics incrementally without tutorials. The game's paradigm (puzzle) prioritizes spatial reasoning and logical deduction; visual design supports this through clear enemy distinctions and unambiguous goal states (locked/unlocked chest, alive/dead player, enemy/egg transformation).
